title: Question about symbols
wikitext:
[]: Question about symbols             Quote Post by IkariRei » Wed Jul 14, 2010 8:52 am Never payed attention but someone is interested and i'm asking here since i never found any info on it. Do these symbols mean anything at all

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Maka » Wed Jul 14, 2010 8:57 am It's part of the Dead Sea Scrolls. There's info on how that was discovered here : http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:OP_B

[]:             Quote Post by IkariRei » Wed Jul 14, 2010 9:44 am Thank you. :)

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:21 pm Maka wrote: It's part of the Dead Sea Scrolls. There's info on how that was discovered here : http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:OP_B

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:25 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: I agree, I was going to say Sanskirt or Ancient Hebrew. It is a logical answer.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:28 pm Allemann wrote: They're made up signs for the show. They don't mean anything.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:35 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Well they closely resemble the aforementioned languages.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:55 pm Allemann wrote: Those are not languages but writing systems. And they definitely don't resemble Sanskrit and the Hebrew alphabet. Even if they do, they can't resemble them both at the same time.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:04 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: I said or, and I said Ancient Hebrew, much older than what you posted there.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:14 pm Allemann wrote: Paleo-hebrew? Not even close. It's a writing system used by the FAR. It's fictional as Klingon.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:22 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Well it resembles something I have seen before. Still, you have to remember dude, we humans are not as creative as we would like to think we are, most of our languages are based off of older ones. Even fake made up languages end up borrowing symbols or sounds from ones used past or present, so it can be easy for one with a hazy memory to make a mistake.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:30 pm Allemann wrote: We're talking here about writing systems . Remember the Saussurian two-tiered concept of the linguistic sign. Graphemes and sounds are signifiers. They only give external form to language. Languages are in human minds.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:36 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Writing systems too, they all are based off of older languages for the most part.

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:37 pm Allemann wrote: Writing system =/= language.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:19 am Looks like a halfassed imitation of the kind of thing you'll find if you search for occult/alchemical secret writing systems, such as "enochian" or "angelic script"
summary:
[]: Question about symbols             Quote Post by IkariRei » Wed Jul 14, 2010 8:52 am Never payed attention but someone is interested and i'm asking here since i never found any info on it. Do these symbols mean anything at all

[]: Re: Question about symbols             Quote Post by Maka » Wed Jul 14, 2010 8:57 am It's part of the Dead Sea Scrolls. There's info on how
